// 01 — About

Namaste — I'm Gunjan

I hold a Master's in Data Analytics from UIS and bring a full-stack data skillset — from wrangling raw pipelines and building predictive models to fine-tuning LLMs and shipping VR data experiences. I don't just analyze; I architect, engineer, and deliver.

Professionally, I've built predictive models, automated pipelines, and driven data strategy for Fortune 500 e-commerce clients. I'm now looking to go deeper — building scalable ML systems, deep learning models, and AI products that solve meaningful problems.

I bring a rare mix of a strong computer science foundation (B.Tech, NIT Hamirpur), hands-on industry experience, and a design sensibility from years of UX/UI and graphic design work. I speak English, Hindi, Maithili, Urdu, and some Arabic.

// 02 — Experience

Where I've Worked

University of Illinois at Springfield
Graduate Research Assistant
Jun 2025 – Aug 2025 · Springfield, IL
  • Collaborated with faculty on VR as a tool for data accessibility, contributing to the design of an immersive U.S. Census data visualization system on Meta Quest 3.
  • Developed interactive prototypes demonstrating how VR supports teaching and improved interpretation of socioeconomic metrics.
Tredence Analytics Pvt. Ltd.
Data Analyst
Jun 2022 – Jun 2024 · Bangalore, India
  • Key member of the offshore Mobile App Marketing Analytics Team for a leading Fortune 500 US e-commerce client; delivered daily insights through statistical analysis, app performance reviews, and predictive modeling.
  • Built a churn model that increased Android active users by 0.37%, resulting in an estimated $4M monthly revenue boost.
  • Automated weekly reporting using Apache Airflow, Dashboard APIs, Python and MS Office, cutting completion time by over 60%.
  • Performed advanced forecasting with Meta Prophet for installs, MAU/WAU, revenue, and retention for FY25 — achieving 6% improvement in forecast accuracy and boosting projected ROI by 2%.

// 03 — Projects

Things I've Built

VR · Data Visualization · Unity
Immersive US Census Data Visualization
A VR app on Meta Quest 3 that renders 3,100+ interactive 3D data points of US Census data (income, population, poverty) with dynamic labeling and gradient coloring by county.
Unity Meta Quest 3 C# OpenXR XR Toolkit
NLP · LLM · Fine-tuning
Formal Speech → Gen Z Meme Language
Fine-tuned FLAN-T5-Base with QLoRA (4-bit) on 28k formal-to-slang pairs. Injected 210 custom emoji/slang tokens, slashing OOV rate from 18% to 5% and outperforming LSTM seq2seq baselines on BLEU & ROUGE-L.
FLAN-T5 QLoRA HuggingFace WandB Google Colab
⚡ Google Colab · GPU T4
Time Series · Deep Learning
Time Series Forecasting (ETTh Dataset)
End-to-end oil temperature prediction system. Trained and compared LSTM, 1D CNN, and N-BEATS models — achieving a best test MAE of 0.76°C vs 1.63°C baseline, a 53% accuracy improvement.
N-BEATS LSTM 1D CNN PyTorch Python
⚡ Google Colab
Computer Vision · Segmentation
Image Segmentation with U-Net
U-Net model with transfer learning (VGG16 encoder), data augmentation, and increased depth. Optimized MeanIoU via systematic ablation study comparing architectures.
U-Net VGG16 TensorFlow Keras
⚡ Google Colab
CNN · Classification · Transfer Learning
RPS Image Classification (CNN)
CNN classifier for Rock/Paper/Scissors images achieving ~99% validation accuracy using DenseNet121 transfer learning and extensive data augmentation.
DenseNet121 TensorFlow Keras Data Augmentation
⚡ Google Colab
ML · Game Design · Decision Trees
Guess the Animal (ML Game)
ML-based 20-questions-style game using the AWA2 dataset. Compared SVM, Neural Networks, and Random Forests — ultimately deploying a decision tree classifier for optimal interactive game performance.
Scikit-Learn AWA2 Dataset Decision Trees Python
Web App · NLP · Audio Synthesis
Text-Based Video Synthesis
Web app converting text scripts into videos by integrating TTS, automated image retrieval, and Gentle forced aligner for word-level audio-visual synchronization. Output via FFmpeg pipeline.
Python FFmpeg TTS Gentle Aligner Web

Several projects were developed in Google Colab notebooks. Screenshots and experiment reports (tracked via Weights & Biases) are available on request — feel free to reach out.
